steps.title: Welcome to Libre
steps.body: Libre turns programming books into runnable lessons. The rail on the left is home base — Library, Discover, Trees, and Playground. Let's walk through the parts of the app that aren't obvious.

steps.title: Your library
steps.body: Books you've installed live here. Click a cover to start reading. Your progress is preserved per book, so you can keep three of them in flight without losing your place in any one.

steps.title: The course tree
steps.body: Once a book is open, the sidebar shows every chapter and lesson. The dot next to each lesson is your status — empty, in progress, or completed. Click any lesson to jump there.

steps.title: Audiobook narration
steps.body: Reading lessons can play as audiobook-quality narration. Tap the speaker to start. The cursor follows along paragraph by paragraph, and pausing freezes both the audio and the highlight.

steps.title: The editor
steps.body: Coding lessons drop you into a real editor with the language's compiler running locally. Press Run to execute your code against the lesson's tests. Failures show up in the panel below, with the assertion that broke.

steps.title: Blocks mode
steps.body: If writing from scratch is too much friction, switch to Blocks. The lesson becomes a drag-and-drop puzzle. Pick the right pieces, and the same tests run against the assembled code.

steps.title: Tracks
steps.body: Don't know where to start? Tracks are curated linear paths — pick an outcome (e.g. Front-End Engineer, Smart-Contract Developer) and follow the sequence. Every step lands on a real lesson in one of your installed books. Scroll the catalogue to fly through your options.

steps.title: Discover more books
steps.body: Discover is the full catalog. One click installs a book into your library. Once it's pulled down, everything you read or practice keeps working offline.

steps.title: You're set
steps.body: Daily reading and practice build a streak. The chip up here tracks it, plus XP earned today and shields you can spend to freeze a missed day. Pick a book, pick a tree, or browse Discover. Have fun.
